sentence: Never before had the city experienced such a devastating flood
hint: Negative adverb + had + subject + past participle
explanation: Inversion after <Never before>: auxiliary <had> comes before the subject.

sentence: Not only did she win the award but she also broke the record
hint: Not only + auxiliary + subject, but also + clause
explanation: <Not only ... but also> with inversion in the first clause.

sentence: Rarely does one encounter such generosity in modern society
explanation: Inversion after <Rarely>: <does> comes before the subject <one>.

sentence: So profound was the impact that the entire industry was transformed
hint: So + adjective + was + subject + that clause
explanation: Inversion with <So ... that>: <So profound was the impact>.

sentence: Under no circumstances should confidential information be shared externally
hint: Under no circumstances + modal + subject + verb
explanation: Inversion after <Under no circumstances>: <should> before subject.

sentence: Only after extensive research did the team discover the root cause
hint: Only after + noun + auxiliary + subject + verb
explanation: Inversion after <Only after>: <did> comes before the subject.

sentence: Little did they realise the consequences of their decision
explanation: Inversion after <Little>: <did> comes before the subject.

sentence: Such was the demand that the company had to increase production
explanation: Inversion with <Such ... that>: <Such was the demand>.

sentence: No sooner had the announcement been made than protests erupted
hint: No sooner + had + subject + past participle + than
explanation: <No sooner ... than> with inversion: <had the announcement been made>.

sentence: At no point during the investigation was any evidence of fraud found
hint: At no point + was + subject + past participle
explanation: Inversion after <At no point>: <was> comes before the subject.

sentence: Scarcely had we begun the meeting when the fire alarm went off
hint: Scarcely + had + subject + past participle + when
explanation: <Scarcely ... when> with inversion: <Scarcely had we begun>.

sentence: Only by working together can we overcome these challenges
hint: Only by + gerund + modal + subject + verb
explanation: Inversion after <Only by>: <can> comes before the subject.
